outcome_summary: Followed the original 'closest above 80 µs' rule and picked headline shape (M=1, K=4096, N=4096). The inline-PTX kernel was hand-tuned for (M=32, K=5376, N=5376), so on M=1 it pads to M=16 and runs 270 µs vs 124 µs torch._scaled_mm. AVO chased a 'small-M decode fast path' rabbit hole, regressed in Step 2, reverted, used 2 of 3 Track 1 steps before being archived. Also produced the verified CuTeDSL/cutlass FP8 atom-availability diagnosis (no warp-level FP8 atom on sm_120 in cutlass-dsl 4.3.4; tcgen05 atoms are sm_100f-only). That diagnosis was kept and informed all later campaigns.
